topRanked.treatmentTitle: Choanolaimus sparsiporus Leduc & Zhao, 2025, sp. nov.
topRanked.articleTitle: Three new nematode species (Chromadorida: Selachinematidae) from the continental slope of New Zealand

images.captionText: Fig. 5. Choanolaimus sparsiporus sp. nov., holotype, ♂ (NIWA 181632) A. Anterior body region. B. Posterior body region. C. Entire specimen. Arrows show position of dorsosublateral pore complexes. Scale bar: A = 75 µm; B = 60 µm; C = 200 µm.

topRanked.treatmentTitle: Choanolaimus serieporus Leduc & Zhao, 2025, sp. nov.
topRanked.articleTitle: Three new nematode species (Chromadorida: Selachinematidae) from the continental slope of New Zealand

images.captionText: Fig. 3. Choanolaimus serieporus sp. nov. A. Entire holotype, ♂ (NIWA 181629). B. Entire paratype, ♀ (NIWA 181631). Arrows show groups of dorsosublateral pore complexes. Scale bar = 200 µm.

Fig. 4. Choanolaimus serieporus sp. nov., light micrographs. A–B. Cephalic region of paratype, ♀ (NIWA 181631). A. Surface view. B. Cross-section view. C. Entire paratype, ♂ (NIWA 181630). D. Lateral view of mid-body cuticle of paratype, ♀ (NIWA 181631). E. Proximal portion of posterior testis of paratype, ♂ (NIWA 181630). F. Proximal portion of anterior testis of paratype, ♂ (NIWA 181630). Arrow shows group of dorsosublateral pore complexes. Scale bar: A, B, D = 25 µm; C = 125 µm; E–F = 40 µm.

response.answer:
The distinguishing feature of Choanolaimus sparsiporus is that it belongs to the diorchic group of choanofiles, where the testes are located opposite the rectum. This is different from other diorchic species like Cypoplasma, which have the testes on the same side as the rectum. Therefore, the correct answer is:

**C) Choanolaimus**
